channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: New Research Details AI’s Negative Impact on Game Developers
item.content:encoded:
AI is problematic for experienced game developers, according to a new report.

The topic of AI in gaming is a persistent hot-button issue. As new technology continues to expand, major forces in the industry believe it is the industry’s future. Alternatively, player backlash is strong against games that use AI in development. Even major companies like Sega have faced backlash after it was revealed that AI was used in the development of the forthcoming Crazy Taxi game.

Steam Players May Be Rejecting AI-Assisted Games, Study Suggests

Now, a new report suggests there’s actual data to back up the claim that the use of AI in game development leads to a decline in sales.

A new report from the Game Oracle finds that AI decreases game reviews by approximately 53%. Furthermore, AI usage also affects the number of wishlists a game gets on Steam.

“If ‘good’ studios are using AI, then AI use is catastrophic (-40% to -60% drop in sales)… Overall, our data suggests that for low-quality games, AI makes no difference. But for high-potential games, the “AI Stigma” is real and severely punishes developers who otherwise would have succeeded, it’s stated.

  • More AI News: Epic Games Says Unreal Engine 6 Will Deepen AI Integration

Still, the report concludes that AI should not be avoided in game development as it is a tool. However, it’s not a replacement for hard work and should be approached with caution by those developers who choose to use it.

Recently, the CEO of Epic Games came out against Valve’s mandatory AI disclosure on Steam. He argued that developers have to choose between being marked with the “Scarlet Letter” of AI or longer development times. 

How do you feel about the use of AI in game development? Sound off about this story and all others in our official Discord server.

For more, check out PlayStation’s comments on AI in future development. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Memory Prices Could Surge Another 50% Before End of 2026
item.content:encoded:
Another report suggests that memory prices will continue to soar for the rest of this year. Relief may arrive in 2028. However, another 50% hike could occur in the third quarter of this year.

The latest report comes from Jefferies Equity Research (via WCCFTech). Tech prices across many industries continue to rise. XBOX recently announced further price increases for the XBOX Series family of consoles, which have been on the market for several years. Apple also recently increased prices for several items. Unfortunately, this latest report supports recent Lenovo claims that it will be at least a few years before a new normal emerges.

Another Report Backs Long-Term Memory Price Increases

Per this report, memory prices are expected to rise by 40-50% in the third quarter of this year, then by another 40% in the final fiscal quarter of 2027.

“A 40-45% Year-on-Year price hike is suggested, and the only recovery will be seen by 2028 when ASPs are expected to decline as new capacity, around 15 to 20 percent, comes into play,” the report states.

  • More Hardware News: Lenovo Says Memory Price Hikes May Never Fully Reverse

The report states that the notion of an overblown Chinese DRAM and NAND market is inaccurate. They are not significantly lowering prices, and their primary advantage lies in supply, most of which is directed toward domestic demand.

Insider Gaming will continue to monitor the memory price hikes and report any updates as more information becomes available.

How do you feel about this news? Sound off about this story and all others in our official Discord server.

For more, check out the reported manufacturing cost of the Sony PlayStation 6. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

item.title: OpTic Texas Beats LA Thieves In Revenge Mission to Win CDL Major 4
item.content:encoded:
OpTic Texas has found success in the Call of Duty League for the first real time this season. At Major 4 in Paris, France, the team lifted the trophy on the grandest stage in COD esports history after beating LA Thieves in the ultimate showdown. This marks OpTic’s first chip this season, which comes as the reigning world champion side approaches COD Champs in Las Vegas.

This historic win comes as the Call of Duty League celebrates pulling in the largest LAN crowd ever, with tens of thousands of fans descending upon Paris to watch this revenge story. For a while, it looked like the ending of the last Major would repeat itself, with LA Thieves beating OpTic in the grand final.

Not only did OpTic win the lion’s share of the $365,000 prize, but the team also put an end to LAT’s monumental win streak.

OpTic Texas Wins The Last Major of the Season

OpTic Texas has had a relatively rough year, topping the CDL standings but missing out on any trophies this season. That was a notion laid to rest this weekend, with the team stepping up to the plate and showing resilience to take down LAT, a team on a sixteen-match win streak going into the grand final.

The run OpTic Texas had in Paris saw them push down Boston Breach and G2 Minnesota before falling against LAT in an upper bracket sweep. From the lower run, the team became big-time villains after eliminating the hometown heroes, Paris Gentle Mates, to make it back into the grand final against LA Thieves.

That ultimate series proved to be a redemption story for OpTic Texas. Mere weeks ago, the team fell at the final post against LAT at Major 3, giving rise to the Thieves’ immense turnaround that led to a series win record being broken.

OpTic Texas came out of the gate strong, putting LAT in the 100-point club in the opening Hardpoint on Den. The Thieves quickly recovered and punished OpTic Texas with back-to-back map wins in both SnD (Sake) and Overload (Scar). The Overload was particularly nasty, with LAT turning it around in the second half and pushing to a 7-3 scoreline.

The next round of Hardpoint was almost too close to call, with LAT and OpTic pushing neck and neck until the latter team went the extra mile to hit a 250-243 scoreline to secure another point in the series. Scrap went hard in an attempt to stay afloat, producing a whopping 41 kills against OpTic.

That return didn’t last too long, though, as LAT pushed for another Search win in the next round. OpTic proved the respawn game was where it was going to shine in this series, and the second Overload on Gridlock went firmly in the Green Wall’s favor.

Then, it all came down to the final map, a round of Search on Gridlock. This was exactly how things ended at Major 3, but it was LAT that walked away with the prize after dominating OpTic in that final round.

This time, OpTic Texas took the trophy, leaving LAT flailing. This puts OpTic in a great place ahead of COD Champs, that’s for sure.

Did you check out this weekend’s Call of Duty esports action? Let us know your thoughts on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that League of Legends Classic will be revealed in full soon, and be sure to subscribe to the Insider Gaming newsletter

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

item.title: COD Champs 2026 Bracket Locked in As Final Major Closes Out
item.content:encoded:
This weekend, the largest Call of Duty LAN event in history played out quite nicely, welcoming tens of thousands of fans in Paris, France. It marked the end of the regular season, and, as always, COD esports fans are now looking to that ultimate event of the year, the Call of Duty League Championship.

Earlier today, the COD League revealed the bracket for this year’s COD Championship, revealing who will be facing whom when the action starts in Las Vegas in just a few weeks.

COD Champs Schedule Revealed

In a post on social media, the COD League revealed the opening schedule for the Call of Duty League Championship, set to land in Las Vegas on July 16. The short-form event ends on July 19, but in that team, a $2 million prize pool will be divided amongst this season’s top eight teams.

Here are the opening matches for this year’s COD Champs:

  • OpTic Texas vs. Miami Heretics
  • Paris Gentle Mates vs. Toronto KOI
  • LA Thieves vs. Riyadh Falcons
  • FaZe Vegas vs. G2 Minnesota

Unfortunately, the season didn’t pan out right for four CDL organizations, all of which failed to qualify for COD Champs:

  • Cloud9 New York
  • Boston Breach
  • Vancouver Surge
  • Carolina Royal Ravens

The disparity between the top and bottom spots in the CDL standings this year runs deeper than ever before. At the top of the table (well, in second place), you’ve got LAT, a team with a chip (and maybe one more this weekend) and the longest match win streak in history (16 at the time of writing) this season.

At the other end of the spectrum, you’ve got Cloud9 New York, a team that has produced one of the worst performances we’ve ever seen in Call of Duty esports, winning just four out of 35 matches this season and producing a map win record of just 28%.

The state of play for COD Champs might already be written. Here are my predictions:

  • OpTic Texas (w)
  • Paris Gentle Mates (w)
  • LA Thieves (w)
  • FaZe Vegas (w)

I can see OpTic going on to defeat Paris, while the Thieves x FaZe series goes to the wire, with LAT ultimately taking the win. The winner’s final will be yet another LAT vs. OpTic Texas showdown, but this time, OpTic will be the victor.

The lower bracket will materialize in a way that pushes FaZe Vegas into the grand final, and we’ll see the two biggest brands in the Call of Duty space collide – El Clásico for COD esports.

OpTic Texas will win, securing a triple-header, a back-to-back-to-back trophy lift in Las Vegas, on FaZe’s home turf.

But that’s just my prediction… Perhaps it’s more of a dream than anything else.

Let us know what you think about the COD Champs bracket on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that League of Legends Classic will be revealed in full soon, and be sure to subscribe to the Insider Gaming newsletter

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill
